objective: Inspect the visible field-kit planner and project custom context-specific UI that helps the user understand the next mission tradeoff.
instructions: Reason from the screenshot and supplied game state only.
Patch only the targets listed in the compact DOM contract.
Prefer a replaceHtml patch on synthesis-overlay when a custom projection can combine coverage, budget, weight, and item tradeoffs.
Use the AI projection layer to create a mission lens, swap analysis, budget/weight visualization, or launch rationale that would be hard to pre-code with static warnings.
Do not merely repeat a single threshold; synthesize at least two signals when using synthesis-overlay.
Prefer one or two decisive patches over broad UI rewrites.
Use one primary short note explaining the most important patch.
Prefer notes on synthesis-overlay, goal rows, gear cards, the coach panel, the launch panel, or the mission checklist.
Avoid long notes on tiny targets such as meters, buttons, or the storm clock.
If something is missing, highlight the missing category or best gear card and explain it in the primary note.
If the screen already looks successful, add a concise success note instead of warnings.
